SE0006993184.Short description: This is an autocallable with more than one underlying asset. It has a call barrier and a coupon barrier, which are at different levels, and a risk barrier. The coupon is flat, no memory.
SE0006993184.Type:
Express certificate (Autocall)
1 b according to the MiFID (legal form?)
SE0006993184.How the investment works: The objective is to receive a coupon, even if the worst performing underlying asset remains unchanged or even has a slightly negative performance.
SE0006993184.row_21: The return of the investment depends on the performance of the worst performing underlying asset during the lifetime of the investment.
SE0006993184.row_22: If, on an observation date, all underlying assets are at or above the call barrier the note will redeem early and the nominal amount plus a coupon will be paid on the early redemption date immediately following the observation date.
SE0006993184.row_23: If, on a valuation date, the worst performing underlying asset is at or above the coupon barrier, but below the call barrier, a coupon will be paid on the next interest payment date. The note will not redeem and the maturity will extend at least to the next early redemption date.
SE0006993184.row_24: If, on a valuation date, the worst performing underlying asset is below both the call barrier and the coupon barrier, the note will not redeem and the maturity will extend at least to the next early redemption date without paying any coupon.
SE0006993184.row_25:
On the last observation date there are three possible scenarios:
1. All underlying assets are at or above the coupon barrier. The note will then pay the nominal amount plus a coupon on the redemption date.
2. The worst performing underlying asset is below the coupon barrier, but at or above the risk barrier. The note will then pay the nominal amount on the redemption date.
3. The worst performing underlying asset is below the risk barrier. The note will then pay the nominal amount reduced by the negative performance of the worst performing underlying asset, on the redemption date.

SE0005795911.Short description: This is a credit linked note linked to the number of credit events in a basket. SE0005795911.Type: 1 b according to the MiFID (legal form?)
SE0005795911.How the investment works: The objective is to invest in a credit linked note with a high target redemption amount.
SE0005795911.row_14: The return of the investment depends on the number of credit events in the underlying assets during the observation period. Bankruptcy, failure to pay, restructuring and governmental intervention are examples that could constitute a credit event.

SE0005795911.row_16: If one or more credit event occurs in the underlying assets during the observation period, each credit event will decrease the target redemption amount by a predefined percentage for each underlying asset. SE0005768736.Short description: This is a credit linked note linked to the number of credit events in a basket. The note pays a floating interest, calculated on the adjusted nominal amount. The note has a buffer, meaning that a predefined percentage of credit events doesn´t affect the nominal amount. Meaning, additional credit events will have a bigger impact on the redemption amount than their basket participation. This is a market recovery note.

SE0005768736.row_16: If not more than a predefined percentage of credit events (the buffer) occur in the underlying assets during the observation period, the note will pay the nominal amount on the redemption date. The buffer is defined as a percentage, and in case of a credit event the buffer will decrease by the company's weight in the basket multiplied by 1 minus the market recovery value.
SE0005768736.row_17: If the percentage of credit events exceeds the buffer, each additional credit event will decrease the nominal amount by the company's weight in the basket multiplied by 1 minus the market recovery value multiplied by the nominal amount. The note will pay the adjusted nominal amount on the redemption date.

Currency fluctuations will also affect the performance of the investment. If the positive performance in the underlying asset, on one single day, multiplied by the leverage is 100 or higher, the note will mature and all amount invested will be lost.

col_1.Short description: This is a non-capital protected note with one underlying asset. If, on the valuation date, the underlying asset is below the strike price the note will pay (strike price - closing price), on the redemption date. If, on the valuation date, the underlying asset is at or above the strike price, all amount invested will be lost. Closing price will be determined during an asian period.
col_1.Type: 1 c according to the MiFID (legal form?)
col_1.How the investment works: The objective is to invest in a warrant, which will give a much higher return than the underlying asset, when the price of the underlying asset is falling.
col_1.row_14: The return of the investment depends on how the underlying asset develops during the lifetime of the investment.
col_1.row_15: If, on the valuation date, the closing price of the underlying asset is below the strike price the note will pay the strike price minus the closing price divided by the number of warrants per underlying, on the redemption date. The note does not pay any dividends from the underlying asset.
col_1.row_16: If, on the valuation date, the closing price of the underlying asset is at or above the strike price, all amount invested will be lost.
col_1.row_17: Closing price for the underlying asset will be the average closing price during a predefined period.

col_1.Short description: This is a non-capital protected issue with one underlying asset. The price of the note will increase when the price of the underlying asset increases, and decrease when the price of the underlying asset decreases. If the underlying asset is at or below the stop-loss level, the investment will redeem early and some or all amount invested will be lost. The stop-loss level and the financing level will be adjusted on a daily basis. The value of the note will be adjusted for dividends in the underlying asset. No asian end.
